title: neopanax simplex var. sinclairii
intro: Neopanax simplex var. sinclairii, also known as the Kawakawa, is a plant native to New Zealand that belongs to the family Araliaceae. Traditionally, the leaves of the Kawakawa plant have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Kawakawa include pain relief, improved digestion, and reduced inflammation, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Kawakawa can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for a range of herbal preparations to suit individual needs.
